req.title: Delete patient (soft) (negative)
req.statement: A system-admin shall soft-delete a patient resource; deletion shall not remove history. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers soft-delete of a Patient resource by a system-admin, with history preservation and error paths for non-admin actors. Tests are designed using: EP for the happy-path admin delete; NEG for the 403 permission-denial path; STC for the already-deleted (conflicting state) transition; DT for missing mandatory path parameter; and BVA for the boundary where the patient id is a single-character minimum-length string. Together these cover the positive, negative, and boundary obligations mandated by the task.

tests.name: test_admin_soft_delete_patient_preserves_history
preconditions: A patient resource with a known id exists and is active
actions: GET /Patient/{id} and assert status is 410 Gone with body indicating deleted
GET /Patient/{id}/_history and assert at least two versions exist
expected: GET /Patient/{id}/_history contains original and deletion versions
tests.executable:
def test_admin_soft_delete_patient_preserves_history(admin_client, active_patient):
    patient_id = active_patient["id"]

    delete_resp = admin_client.delete(f"/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert delete_resp.status_code in (200, 204)

    get_resp = admin_client.get(f"/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 410
    body = get_resp.json()
    assert body.get("issue") or body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"

    hist_resp = admin_client.get(f"/Patient/{patient_id}/_history")
    assert hist_resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = hist_resp.json()
    entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 2
    methods = [e.get("request", {}).get("method") for e in entries]
    assert "DELETE" in methods or any(
        e.get("resource", {}).get("meta", {}).get("tag") for e in entries
    )

tests.name: test_non_admin_delete_patient_returns_403
preconditions: A patient resource with a known id exists
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome with security issue
trace: R-HLT-054: error pathway — 403 when not admin
tests.executable:
def test_non_admin_delete_patient_returns_403(practitioner_client, active_patient):
    patient_id = active_patient["id"]

    resp = practitioner_client.delete(f"/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert resp.status_code == 403

    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(
        i.get("severity") in ("error", "fatal") for i in issues
    )

tests.name: test_delete_already_deleted_patient_returns_conflict_or_gone
preconditions: A patient resource with a known id has already been soft-deleted
actions: Ensure patient is soft-deleted via first DELETE call
Issue a second DELETE /Patient/{id} with admin credentials
Assert response status signals conflicting state (409 or 410)
expected: Second DELETE returns 409 Conflict or 410 Gone
Response body contains OperationOutcome describing the conflicting state
trace: R-HLT-054: conflicting state transitions must be tested
tests.executable:
def test_delete_already_deleted_patient_returns_conflict_or_gone(
    admin_client, active_patient
):
    patient_id = active_patient["id"]

    first = admin_client.delete(f"/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert first.status_code in (200, 204)

    second = admin_client.delete(f"/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert second.status_code in (409, 410)

    body = second.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert len(issues) >= 1

tests.name: test_delete_patient_missing_id_returns_405_or_400
actions: DELETE /Patient/ (empty id — boundary of zero-length id)
Assert response is 400, 404, or 405 (method not allowed on collection)
trace: R-HLT-054: missing mandatory elements must be tested
BVA: id length at zero boundary
tests.executable:
def test_delete_patient_missing_id_returns_405_or_400(admin_client):
    resp = admin_client.delete("/Patient/")
    assert resp.status_code in (400, 404, 405)
    assert resp.status_code not in (200, 204)

tests.name: test_delete_patient_single_char_id_boundary
preconditions: A patient resource whose FHIR logical id is exactly one character (minimum valid id length) exists
expected: History endpoint returns bundle with at least one entry for single-char id
tests.executable:
def test_delete_patient_single_char_id_boundary(admin_client, single_char_id_patient):
    patient_id = single_char_id_patient["id"]
    assert len(patient_id) == 1

    del_resp = admin_client.delete(f"/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert del_resp.status_code in (200, 204)

    hist_resp = admin_client.get(f"/Patient/{patient_id}/_history")
    assert hist_resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = hist_resp.json()
    assert len(bundle.get("entry", [])) >= 1

tests.name: test_delete_patient_without_auth_token_returns_401
preconditions: A patient resource with a known id exists
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome or WWW-Authenticate header is present
tests.executable:
def test_delete_patient_without_auth_token_returns_401(unauthenticated_client, active_patient):
    patient_id = active_patient["id"]

    resp = unauthenticated_client.delete(f"/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert resp.status_code == 401

    has_www_auth = "WWW-Authenticate" in resp.headers
    has_outcome = resp.json().get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    assert has_www_auth or has_outcome
